reviews.review_msg: Assignments are very simple to do, and TAs give out  A's easily. As this course is blended, her lectures are posted online BUT if you don't stay on track it can be very easy to fall behind (speaking from experience) and end up watching the lectures last min. I did bad on the first midterm 53% but the final was easy and got 94%, so don't worry a lot

A very happy and passionate professor. Classroom was flipped so we had to come to class to work on our group assignment every week, expected to have taught the material to ourselves. She would post lectures that would mostly explain things clearly, probably ~30% of the time I had to find outside material to explain things. Made learning easy!

Heather Bliss is one of the best profs ive met, she actually cares and wants her students to succeed. During the exam i forgot concept and asked her about it and she legit taught it right there. The exams itself were not bad at all, as long as you revise the weekly lecture notes you'll be fine. make sure you choose a good group for the weekly clues

Heather is a very kind prof. Taking accountability is a 50/50 with her lectures being online then coming to class having to submit assignments every week. I reccomend saving the week's clue (assignments) material if unfamiliar. Another good thing is to find a solid group throughout the course rather than having to jump from group to group each week

Prof Bliss is kind and understanding, but I didn't like the formatting of the course. She doesn't use the lectures to teach but makes us do mandatory group assignments(clues). If you miss a lecture and don't submit the clue, then you get a ZERO. I felt like she didn't teach much in the course; she made us watch prerecorded online lectures.
Very kind and considerate when it came to assignments and workload. Loved the online lectures in the class I took and thought that class time being used for assignments was nice.

Heather is the best! Her class is super straightforward, and it's easy to get a good grade. Make sure your group is good, since you most likely will work with them for the rest of the semester. The two exams are easy. For the first one, make sure to study IPA. Overall, listen to the lectures and go over the notes a couple times before the exams.

Dr. Bliss is a great prof. She's passionate and makes a great effort to keep her lecture material engaging and relevant. Learning is done through reasonably-sized posted lecture recordings + readings. Grading is fair and weekly in-class assignments are extremely helpful and set you up for success towards exams. Would recommend.
Great Proffesor! She is very nice and willing to help, and believes a lot in particiaption and getting homework done in class, which was actually great.
Took LING 100 with her in the Spring. Very easy course. Heather was very sweet and was a pretty good intro to linguistics. Overall great experience and a very nice professor

give her a raise. Heather is awesome and is flexible with your needs. Truly here to teach and make sure we understand content. She connects well with students and just wants the best for us. She's so smart and knows exactly what to say all the time. Gives great examples and explanations.

topic was indigenous languages in canada! eye-opening course and i enjoyed it, despite it not being something i'm wholeheartedly interested in. heather is very nice and approachable, her TA gave good feedback as well! stay on top of the weekly journals/assignments. lecture notes are extremely helpful with studying and following along! [spring 2022]

Take every class you can with Heather! The sweetest prof I have ever had and you can tell she cares about her students. 282W is a writing intensive course so there was a lot of material but Heather was always encouraging us to ask for help and truly wanted us to do well.

Heather is amazing! Super nice, chill, and passionate about linguistics especially language revitalization. Her lectures are high quality but also content-heavy. As for the course itself, LING282W is a writing-heavy course, you will be doing writing *every week* and get marks docked for analysis, clarity, coherence, presentation, etc. It's hard.
Truly a one of a kind prof who actually cares about her students

Prof Bliss is a kind and passionate prof! She really tries to make lectures interesting and engaging, and does a good job of incorporating popular culture and current slang. Her lecture notes are easy to follow and she encourages a lot of in-class participation and activities which is a nice way to connect to the material.

She is very nice and gives answer keys for assignments and the midterm which was helpful. Tutorials were awesome and helpful. The final was very much like the midterm format but heavy on syntax/morphology/semantics. She kind of talks fast in class, but her lectures are straightforward. Taking her for LING282W next term.
Very nice and always makes sure that you understand the material.
Best prof in the linguistics department. She knows what she is teaching. A real gem.

I think she's a great prof. She loves her line of work and shows it in her lectures. Exams and assignments based on lectures. Must understand topics well to get a better grade. A solid intro course if you do not know what to take. Been taking another course with her the consecutive sem. Clear grade criteria, interesting lectures, simple assignments
Exams are fair, material wasn't too hard. Good prof, passionate about her subject and willing to help students

Dr. Bliss is an amazing professor who knows her subject well and is passionate about teaching to young minds. The grading was not too hard, I mostly had issues due to not being familiar with IPA (which you should learn before taking this class btw). Overall an amazing class and helped me to reignite my passion for linguistics.
Fair,clear,passionate about her field, available to students.

Dr. Bliss is an awesome professor who is very knowledgeable about her field of work. The TA's in the class are super helpful, and along with Dr. Bliss try their best to help you understand many complex linguistic theories. It is definitely hard, but worth it if you are at all interested in learning linguistics. Pro Tip: learn IPA before class start

Although I didn't enjoy the class itself, I found Heather to be a really great prof. You can tell that she's really passionate about linguistics and really encourages the class to share their ideas, too. You can basically not show up to her class and just refer to the online slides she posts and you'll be good. Her tests are also really fair.

Favourite professor so far. No tutorials but she encourages participation and discussion during lecture. Genuinely nice person and tries to remember your name. She's clearly very passionate about linguistics and her exams are straightforward - she even states her exams are to support our learning. Really want to take another course with her :)
